tagline: Work with Twenty CRM objects from the command line.

global_flags.name: credential
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Twenty credential by name.

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Alias for selecting a saved Twenty connector credential.

global_flags.name: limit
global_flags.summary: Limit records returned by implemented list commands.

global_flags.name: preview
global_flags.summary: Build a reverse ETL preview for implemented write commands before approval/execution.

commands.summary: List Twenty attachments records

flags.name: limit
flags.summary: Maximum records to read for this command.

commands.summary: Get one Twenty attachments record by ID

flags.name: id
flags.summary: Twenty attachments record ID.

commands.notes: Generic JSON direct_read execution and output_policy support are outside S6; this command documents the fixed Twenty get-by-id endpoint but is not executable yet.

commands.summary: Create a Twenty attachments record
commands.risk: Creates a Twenty attachments record through the REST API.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: name
flags.summary: Top-level name field for the Twenty attachments record.

flags.name: position
flags.summary: Top-level position numeric field for the Twenty attachments record.

commands.summary: Update a Twenty attachments record
commands.risk: Updates an existing Twenty attachments record through the REST API.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: id
flags.summary: Twenty attachments record ID.

flags.name: name
flags.summary: Top-level name field for the Twenty attachments record.

flags.name: position
flags.summary: Top-level position numeric field for the Twenty attachments record.

commands.summary: Batch-create Twenty attachments records
commands.risk: Bulk creates Twenty attachments records through the REST API.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL batch writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: Execution requires reverse ETL records with a top-level records array. CLI scalar flag coercion for records arrays is deferred; no generic JSON or raw write flag is exposed in S6.

commands.summary: Delete a Twenty attachments record
commands.risk: Destructively deletes a Twenty attachments record through the REST API.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL deletes require plan, preview, approval, execute, and typed --confirm destructive.

flags.name: id
flags.summary: Twenty attachments record ID to delete.

examples: pm twenty attachments delete --id rec_fixture --preview --confirm destructive --json
